Petition For Removal Of Administrator And Appointment Of Successor Administrator
Off Calendar Jul-05-2016 Continued To Aug-09-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Bridgman for and with (administrator) Janice Rogers. Scott Bassin for Javier Reyes. Dimitri Mims in Propria Persona.

Mr. Bridgman reported that the TIC agreement was presented to the Mims, that there has been no feedback, that they were to have hired an attorney, that they are altering their position, that Ms. Rogers has agreed to sell the entire house and that they may need cooperation from the Mims family. Mr. Bassin requested that one of the following happen: that they be granted a short continuance to list the property, that Mr. Reyes is prepared to purchase the 1/3 interest, that a petition for distribution in kind be filed or that Ms. Rogers be removed. Mr. Bridgman stated that this is the first he is hearing as to the buyout by Mr. Reyes. Mr. Mims reported that his family has been in discussions about him purchasing the top unit or going in with his cousin, that they are leaning strongly on Mr. Mims purchasing it and that he is working to obtain counsel. The Court stated that there are a lot of things in flux, that the courts only interest is to get this done, that maybe if there are competing bidders they can get more for the property, that the declaration of Mr. Reyes' broker indicates that he wants to purchase at a low price, that they can do a distribution in kind, that the Court will give them another chance since Mr. Reyes & Mr. Mims are showing interest, that it wants to see a plan in which things will get done and that it is not sure that selling the entire property would be any easier. Mr. Bridgman stated that they may need a partition action. The Court responded that a partition action could happen outside of Probate, that they now have negotiating to do and that if it is not accomplished they will need an amended petition to get probate completed. The Petition for removal of administrator and appointment of successor administrator was continued to August 9, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court expects the parties to start working on this immediately.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Bridgman for and with Janice Rogers. Scott Bassin for and with Javier Reyes. William Taylor for and with Kenneth Gregory.

The Court stated that two petitions have been filed, that as to the administrators petition the court has not received courtesy copies, that there is no proof of service, that they are asking in this case to reform an order in another case, that they want the court to approve condomization which needs to go through city procedures, and that the courts interest is in getting this done. Mr. Bridgman stated that they filed a petition in a parallel case, that it is not in the court's system yet but that they expect to get a hearing date today, that the property has an undivided 1/3 interest between 3 people and that that fastest way to sell it is to get deeds. The Court stated that this is not before it in a way to deal with the merits. Mr. Bridgman stated that they would like to get a continuance date to the same hearing date as in the Ann Thompson case and that the petition is not yet served. Mr. Taylor stated that they support the current administrator and object to Javier Reyes being appointed. Mr. Bassin stated that they served everyone necessary, that the proof of service filed by Mr. Bridgman is the first time they have seen addresses for certain heirs, that the Snelgro's are not heirs in this estate, that in regards to the objection to Mr. Reyes being appointed they just want to see the estate closed, that they agree with the court regarding the condomization, that they would be happy to see the Public Administrator step in and get this closed and that there has been no realistic movement towards getting this closed. Mr. Bridgman stated that 113 Pierce is not the address of Lamarcus Gregory & Kenneth Gregory, that this case was a disaster because notice was not given early on that that the court let it slide. The Court stated that re-fighting battles and pointing fingers will not advance this case, that notices should have gone out, that it is skeptical that the proposal of the administrator will work, that it will continue the matter one more time so that the administrator's proposal is properly before the court, that it will order that Ms. Rogers provide an alternate plan (sale of 1/3 interest or distribution in kind), that all of the parties have a right to getting this finished and that they may then be in a position to make an order to get this done. The petition for removal of administrator and appointment of successor administrator and the petition of administrator for reformation of court order and approval of plan of sale of property were continued to March 1, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and that it will allow the matter in the other estate to be set for that date also and orders that Ms. Rogers file papers with an alternative plan for a sale of 1/3 interest or distribution in kind to those entitled.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-06-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Scott Basin for Javier Reyes. Dennis Vann for Janice Rogers and Carol Gregory.

Ms. Gregory addressed the Order To Show Cause stating that the accounting wasn't in order, that Ms. Rogers made a mess of it, and that it needed to be looked over. Mr. Vann stated that Ms. Gregory came into his office after the Order to Show Cause issued, that he gave her all the records to review, that she couldn't point out what was wrong except two missing mortgage payments that he missed, that she wouldn't approve or sign the accounting, and that the information from Ms. Rogers and Mrs. Mims was not complete because they didn't think they would ever be in Court on the Pierce Street property. Discussions regarding the Broad Street property and the accounting period were held. Mr. Vann stated the accounting period ran through December 2008. Mr. Bassin stated that his client can't pay his own rent, that this has been ongoing for a year and a half, that by the time the property is sold his client's portion will be slim, that the accounting should be allowed not approved, and that he wants the property distributed so it could be sold. Mr. Vann stated that he attempted to contact Ms. Snelgro's children with no response and that he didn't know if she died testate or intestate. Mr. Vann presented additional argument and stated that if there is only a lien on the Broad Street property he believes there won't be enough money to cover the liens. Mr. Bassin concurred; liens should be on both properties. Ms. Rogers stated that she was trying to secure a loan to pay Javier off and that she was trying to assist two brothers. Kenneth Gregory, Amanda Riddle and Alma Mims all stated their concerns for the record. Additional arguments presented. Mr. Vann stated that the decedent left two disabled children and they are trying to keep their housing. Submitted.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.
